title: Multi-step plan executor with rollback
description: Executes a sequence of interdependent steps (e.g., git pull → build → test → deploy) with automatic rollback on any step failure, logging each step's output and state.
motivation: Users frequently ask me to perform complex multi-step operations (deployments, migrations, refactors) where a single failure can corrupt state. Currently I must manually orchestrate each step and handle errors ad-hoc, leading to brittle scripts and unrecoverable states.
implementationApproach: Build a pipeline executor that wraps each step in a try/except block, captures stdout/stderr, and on failure runs a pre-defined rollback function (e.g., git revert, restore backup) before reporting the error with full context.

title: Context-aware file patcher with semantic diff
description: Applies code changes by understanding the surrounding context (function boundaries, indentation, imports) rather than just line numbers, and generates a semantic diff showing what changed and why.
motivation: Current str_replace/edit_file tools are fragile: they fail when line numbers shift after edits, or when whitespace differs. Users report 'patch failed' errors that require manual intervention. A semantic patcher would understand code structure and adapt to minor variations.
implementationApproach: Parse the target file into an AST (using tree-sitter or language-specific parser), locate the target code block by signature (function name, class name, pattern match), apply the change, and regenerate the file with proper formatting. Show a human-readable diff with change reasons.

title: Autonomous web form filler with visual verification
description: Combines browser automation with screenshot analysis to fill and submit web forms: extracts form fields via DOM, fills them using context from user instructions, takes a screenshot before submit, and asks user to confirm or correct.
motivation: Users often ask me to 'fill out this form' or 'submit this application' but current browser tools lack form awareness. I frequently fill wrong fields, miss required inputs, or submit without validation. This capability would make web automation reliable and safe.
implementationApproach: Use browser_extract to get all input/select/textarea elements with labels and placeholders, map them to user-provided data, fill using browser_type, take a screenshot via browser_screenshot, analyze with analyze_image to verify correctness, then submit only after user confirmation.

title: Autonomous web form filler with visual verification
description: Combines browser automation with screenshot analysis to fill and submit web forms: extracts form fields via DOM, fills them using context from user instructions, takes a screenshot before submit, and asks user to confirm or correct.
motivation: Users often ask me to 'fill out this form' or 'submit this application' but current browser tools lack form awareness. I frequently fill wrong fields, miss required inputs, or submit without validation. This capability would make web automation reliable and safe.
implementationApproach: Build a pipeline that uses browser_extract to get form structure, browser_type to fill fields, screenshot + analyze_image to verify correctness, then ask_human for confirmation before browser_click to submit.

title: Self-healing code repair with test-driven validation
description: When a tool or script fails, automatically diagnose the error, propose a fix, apply it, run the existing test suite, and revert if tests fail — all without human intervention.
motivation: I frequently encounter runtime errors, broken imports, or API changes in my own codebase. Currently I must manually diagnose, patch, and test. This capability would let me autonomously recover from failures and maintain stability.
implementationApproach: Chain self_diagnose → self_patch_file (or self_atomic_modify) → self_run_tests → if tests fail, self_heal (revert) and report; if pass, confirm success. Use self_diff to review changes before applying.

title: Multi-step plan executor with rollback
description: Executes a sequence of dependent steps (e.g., clone repo → install deps → run tests → deploy), checkpointing state after each step, and automatically rolling back to the last good checkpoint if any step fails.
motivation: Users ask me to perform complex multi-step workflows (e.g., 'deploy this app', 'migrate the database'). A single failure in the middle can leave the system in an inconsistent state. This capability makes multi-step operations safe and reliable.
implementationApproach: Create a plan executor that uses create_plan to define steps, then executes each step with bash_execute/python_execute, captures a git snapshot or filesystem backup before each step, and on failure restores the last checkpoint using git_operations or file restore.

title: Autonomous Multi-Step Workflow with Checkpoint Recovery
description: Executes complex multi-step plans (e.g., 'install dependencies, run tests, deploy') with automatic checkpointing at each step, rollback on failure, and resumption from the last successful checkpoint.
motivation: Users frequently encounter failures mid-way through long tasks (e.g., test suite fails after code changes, deployment halts due to network issues). Current tools require manual re-execution from scratch, wasting time and risking inconsistent state. This prevents cascading failures and enables safe, unattended execution.
implementationApproach: Wrap the existing `create_plan` and `sandbox_execute` tools with a state machine that snapshots filesystem state (via git_operations) and logs step results. On failure, restore to last checkpoint and optionally retry or ask human for guidance.

title: Semantic Code Patch Generator with Test Validation
description: Analyzes a codebase for a given issue (e.g., bug, missing feature), generates a semantically correct patch using context-aware diffing, then automatically runs the test suite to validate the patch before applying it.
motivation: Direct file editing often introduces subtle bugs (e.g., incorrect variable scope, broken imports) that are only caught later. Users need a tool that understands code semantics, not just text, and validates changes against existing tests to prevent regressions.
implementationApproach: Combine `self_read_file`, `search_files`, and `python_execute` (for AST parsing) to understand code structure. Use `self_patch_file` with a diff algorithm that respects syntax boundaries. Then run `self_run_tests` and rollback if tests fail.

title: Visual Regression Detector for Web UI Changes
description: Compares screenshots of web pages before and after code changes, highlighting visual differences (layout shifts, color changes, missing elements) and flagging them as potential regressions.
motivation: Users often make CSS or DOM changes that break the visual appearance of a web app. Current tools only check functional correctness (tests) but miss visual regressions. This fills a critical gap for frontend development.
implementationApproach: Use `browser_screenshot` or `visual_screenshot` to capture baseline and current states. Apply pixel-diff algorithms (e.g., SSIM) and overlay bounding boxes on differences. Report results via `ask_human` or store in memory for review.

title: Autonomous Multi-Step Workflow with Checkpoint Recovery
description: Executes complex multi-step plans (e.g., 'fix bug X, update docs, deploy to staging, run E2E tests') with automatic checkpointing at each step. If a step fails, the system rolls back to the last successful checkpoint, logs the failure, and retries with adjusted parameters or asks for user guidance.
motivation: Users often need to chain multiple operations (code edit → test → deploy → verify) but failures in later steps waste time and can corrupt state. This prevents cascading failures, reduces manual oversight, and enables truly autonomous long-running tasks.
implementationApproach: Wrap the existing `create_plan` and `sandbox_execute` tools with a state machine that snapshots filesystem state (via git_operations) and tool outputs after each step, then implements conditional rollback logic using `self_heal` and `ask_human` for unresolvable failures.

title: Semantic Code Patch Generator with Test Validation
description: Analyzes a codebase for a given issue (e.g., bug, missing feature), generates a semantically correct patch using context-aware diffing, then automatically runs the test suite to validate the patch before applying it.
motivation: Direct file editing often introduces subtle bugs (e.g., incorrect variable scope, broken imports) that are only caught later. Users need a tool that understands code semantics, not just text, and validates changes against existing tests to prevent regressions.
implementationApproach: Combine `search_files`/`read_file` for codebase analysis, `python_execute` to parse AST and generate context-aware diffs, then use `sandbox_execute` to run tests in an isolated environment before applying the patch via `self_patch_file`.

title: Visual Regression Detector for Web UI Changes
description: Compares screenshots of web pages before and after code changes, highlighting visual differences (layout shifts, color changes, missing elements) and flagging them as potential regressions.
motivation: Users often make CSS or DOM changes that break the visual appearance of a web app. Current tools only check functional correctness (tests) but miss visual regressions. This fills a critical gap for frontend development.
implementationApproach: Use `browser_screenshot`/`visual_screenshot` to capture baseline and post-change screenshots, then leverage `analyze_image` or a Python pixel-diff library (via `python_execute`) to generate a diff overlay and report discrepancies.

title: Autonomous Multi-Step Workflow with Checkpoint Recovery
description: Executes complex multi-step plans (e.g., 'fix bug X, update docs, deploy to staging, run E2E tests') with automatic checkpointing at each step. If a step fails, the system rolls back to the last successful checkpoint, logs the failure, and retries with adjusted parameters or asks for user guidance.
motivation: Users often need to chain multiple operations (code edit → test → deploy → verify) but failures in later steps waste time and can corrupt state. This prevents cascading failures, reduces manual oversight, and enables truly autonomous long-running tasks.
implementationApproach: Extend the existing `create_plan` tool with a state machine that snapshots file system state (via git_operations) before each step, and on failure, restores the snapshot and either retries with modified parameters or escalates to `ask_human`.

title: Semantic Code Patch Generator with Test Validation
description: Analyzes a codebase for a given issue (e.g., bug, missing feature), generates a semantically correct patch using context-aware diffing, then automatically runs the test suite to validate the patch before applying it.
motivation: Direct file editing often introduces subtle bugs (e.g., incorrect variable scope, broken imports) that are only caught later. Users need a tool that understands code semantics, not just text, and validates changes against existing tests to prevent regressions.
implementationApproach: Combine `search_files` and `read_file` to gather context, use `python_execute` with AST parsing to generate a diff, apply it via `str_replace` or `edit_file`, then run `self_run_tests` or `bash_execute` with pytest; roll back via `git_operations` if tests fail.

title: Visual Regression Detector for Web UI Changes
description: Compares screenshots of web pages before and after code changes, highlighting visual differences (layout shifts, color changes, missing elements) and flagging them as potential regressions.
motivation: Users often make CSS or DOM changes that break the visual appearance of a web app. Current tools only check functional correctness (tests) but miss visual regressions. This fills a critical gap for frontend development.
implementationApproach: Use `browser_screenshot` or `visual_screenshot` to capture before/after images, then leverage `analyze_image` or a Python script (via `python_execute`) with image comparison libraries (e.g., pixelmatch) to generate a diff overlay and report changes.
